nodes.name: 气候变化
nodes.summary: 长期改变水文节律与生态系统稳定性的全球变化因素。

nodes.name: Yellow-breasted boatbill
nodes.summary: Yellow-breasted boatbill（Yellow-breasted boatbill，学名 Machaerirhynchus flaviventer），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联7个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括热带雨林，主要威胁包括气候变化。

nodes.name: 新幾內亞島
nodes.summary: 新幾內亞島是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联312种鸟类，包括南方鶴鴕、鵲鵝、细斑树鸭等312种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 约翰·古尔德
nodes.summary: 约翰·古尔德是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联71种鸟类，包括绿棉凫、凍原豆雁、黄腹角雉等71种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 南半球
nodes.summary: 南半球是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联7种鸟类，包括Grey petrel、Great-winged petrel、Scissor-tailed nightjar等7种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 雨林
nodes.summary: 雨林是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联99种鸟类，包括叢塚雉、鳳冠孔雀雉、Amboyna cuckoo-dove等99种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 热带雨林
nodes.summary: 结构复杂且冠层完整的高生物多样性森林。

nodes.name: 约克角半岛
nodes.summary: 约克角半岛是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联35种鸟类，包括尖羽树鸭、巴布亞蟆口鴟、澳大利亚金丝燕等35种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Far North Queensland
nodes.summary: Far North Queensland是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联3种鸟类，包括叢塚雉、Noisy miner、Yellow-breasted boatbill，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: Gallery forest
nodes.summary: Gallery forest是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联206种鸟类，包括Blue-throated piping guan、White-throated piping guan、黑額鳴冠雉等206种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: thumb|225x225px|Simple map of Oceania. thumb|333x333px|An illustration depicting a yellow-breasted boatbill (Machaerirhynchus flaviventer) and a Wallace's fairy-wren (Sipodotus wallacii) perched on a branch. The yellow-breasted boatbill (Machaerirhynchus flaviventer) is a species of bird found in New Guinea and Far North Queensland, Australia. The yellow-breasted boatbill is a species of bird belonging to the Machaerirhynchidae family, of the genus Machaerirhynchus.{{Cite journal|last=Davis|first=W

The yellow-breasted boatbill (Machaerirhynchus flaviventer) is a species of bird found in New Guinea and Far North Queensland, Australia. The yellow-breasted boatbill is a species of bird belonging to the Machaerirhynchidae family, of the genus Machaerirhynchus.{{Cite journal|last=Davis|first=W

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The yellow-breasted boatbill can be found in the southern hemisphere, living in tropical areas all across New Guinea, but is not endemic to any specific country or island. This species has a very large range and it can also be found at the Cape York Peninsula, Australia, having been documented inhabiting the subtropical and tropical rainforests of North Queensland.{{Cite journal|last=Freeman|first=B

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The yellow-breasted boatbill can be found in the southern hemisphere, living in tropical areas all across New Guinea, but is not endemic to any specific country or island. This species has a very large range and it can also be found at the Cape York Peninsula, Australia, having been documented inhabiting the subtropical and tropical rainforests of North Queensland.{{Cite journal|last=Freeman|first=B

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The yellow-breasted boatbill has a wider distribution across the entire island of New Guinea but can be found in higher population densities within North Queensland rainforests. Across all the listed geographical regions, the yellow-breasted boatbill is most commonly found in rainforest areas, where there is a high density of vegetation, typically composed of evergreen trees and shrubbery

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Similarly, the yellow-breasted boatbill can also be found in gallery forests

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: The yellow-breasted boatbill can be found in the southern hemisphere, living in tropical areas all across New Guinea, but is not endemic to any specific country or island. This species has a very large range and it can also be found at the Cape York Peninsula, Australia, having been documented inhabiting the subtropical and tropical rainforests of North Queensland.{{Cite journal|last=Freeman|first=B

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Furthermore, there are currently no major natural threats to the species. However, the boatbill is considered to be among the top 100 terrestrial Australian bird species that is most sensitive to the effects of climate change upon ecosystems across Australia. On account of its conservation status, the yellow-breasted boatbill is a valuable source of information because it can be studied to see what factors contribute to being able to thrive in harmony with humans. Ornithologists can focus on the components that contribute to the yellow-breasted boatbill's survival, as well as those factors which threaten it
